steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures around the blank
steps.content: The blank separates an incomplete noun phrase on the left from a clause on the right. Left: “This type of random, continuous” (noun phrase modifier). Right: “is known as Brownian motion, can be observed...” (beginning with a verb).

steps.title: Apply the non-restrictive relative clause rule
steps.content:
Non-restrictive relative clauses—ones that provide extra information about a noun without restricting its meaning—must be set off by commas on both sides.

Choice B correctly provides the comma before “which,” and the existing comma after “motion” completes the punctuation.

The other choices either omit the required comma, use wrong punctuation, or create sentence fragments.

This matches choice B — B is correct.
